MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Element 26 Environmental Demoltion, Inc. For Summary Judgment Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication, Separate Statement Of Undisputed Facts, Supporting Memorandum And Supporting Evidence And Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Oct-23-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM DARBY, TEL. NO. (510) 589-1586 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT;
ATTY. TYSON ARBUTHNOT OF THE ROGERS JOSEPH O'DONNELL, TEL. NO. 9565-2828 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT;
ATTY. PATRICIA BUCKLEY, TEL. NO. 883-2694 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT WESTERN SURETY.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-COMPLAINANT ELEMENT 26 ENVIRONMENTAL & DEMOLITION, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication is denied in its entirety. First, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether there is an unpaid balance owed to Element 26 for the Crystal Springs project. Pursuant to the Payment Bond, payment is due only for work provided for in the subcontract and for other authorized additions to and modifications of that subcontract. (Bryant Dec. Ex. HH.) Whether all of the work performed and billed by Element 26 on the Crystal Springs project was in conformance with the project specifications, and whether Element 26 was fully paid for the properly billed and approved work that it provided are material facts that are in dispute. (UMF # 168, 173.) Second, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Element was underpaid for work that it performed on the Harry Tracy project. (UMF # 138.) Additionally, there is a triable issue as to whether Element 26 was acting as a subcontractor on the project, and what the terms of the contract were. (UMF # 114, 131, 138.) In light of the existence of these triable issues, Element 26 has failed to show that it is entitled to judgment on any cause of action against any party. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
